description: This file contains Balanda future tense modal constructions using 'nonve'. This modality expresses future ability or possibility and corresponds to the English meaning 'will be able to'.

verb_form.structure: le-VERB (base verb form with future prefix)

affirmative_form.notes: This construction expresses future modal ability

question_form.function: Forms yes/no questions expressing future ability or possibility

constraints.structure: nonve must precede the verb; le- must attach directly to the verb; The verb remains in its base form aside from the le- prefix; No additional tense or aspect markers may co-occur with le-; No negation marker (gba) appears in this file; Questions do not change word order; we? always appears at the end of the clause

output: I will be able to eat food.
I will be able to drink water.
He will be able to sleep.
I will be able to go to Wau.
He will be able to come to the house.
The child will be able to walk in the compound.
He will be able to work in the garden.
We will be able to play.
I will be able to love you.
I will be able to know the truth.
He will be able to think.
I will be able to ask them.
He will be able to tell a story.
I will be able to see you.
He will be able to hear.
I will be able to write a book.
He will be able to read a book.
I will be able to buy a bike.
He will be able to sell food.
I will be able to push the fruit.
He will be able to pull the drum.
He will be able to turn the table.
I will be able to call them.
We will be able to discuss the story.
He will be able to hug the child.
They will be able to fight.
He will be able to protect the child.
He will be able to fall.
He will be able to change.
The tree will be able to darken.
The school will be able to develop.
The child will be able to mature.
He will be able to be in the house.
I will be able to do the work.
I will be able to live in Wau.
I will be able to want water.
He will be able to remain behind.
He will be able to sing.
He will be able to dance.
I will be able to watch the animals.
Will I be able to eat food?
Will I be able to drink water?
Will he be able to sleep?

input: Ne nonve leyai a Wau we?
output: Will I be able to go to Wau?

input: Ni nonve ledhii agbi tiyi we?
output: Will he be able to come to the house?

input: Wiki nonve lenono gbi difo we?
output: Will the child be able to walk in the compound?

input: Ni nonve lemande gbi njü we?
output: Will he be able to work in the garden?
Will we be able to play?
